description_long: This clock tower is a tall, slender structure constructed primarily from red and beige stone or brick, featuring ornate architectural detailing. The tower is divided into several sections, with arched doorways at the base, tall vertical windows in the midsection, and a prominent clock face set into a decorative frame near the top. The roof is steeply pitched and spired, with layered shingles and a pointed finial at the peak. The clock face is circular, with clear numerals and hands, and is surrounded by intricate stonework. The overall design is reminiscent of Victorian Gothic architecture, with pointed arches, buttresses, and decorative masonry. The structure appears weathered, suggesting age and historical significance.
description: A tall, ornate stone clock tower with a spired roof, arched doorways, and a prominent circular clock face set in decorative masonry. The design features red and beige stonework and Gothic architectural details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main arched entrance, tall windows, and the clock face above, with the spired roof clearly visible.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view revealing more of the left side, with the clock face and entrance still visible but the side buttresses more pronounced.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the clock face and entrance centered, similar to the front but with subtle differences in masonry and window arrangement.
description_view_3: View from the right side, showing the clock face and entrance, with more of the right buttresses and side details visible.
attribute_clock_face_design: The clock face is circular with a white background, black Roman numerals marking the hours, and two black hands for hours and minutes. The face is bordered by a decorative stone frame, and the numerals are clearly legible. There are no additional decorative elements within the clock face itself.
